title: Ultrafast Critical Slowing of Spin Dynamics and Emergent Nonequilibrium Fano Interference in Fe3GeTe2
authors: Anupama Chauhan, Sidhanta Sahu, Satyabrata Bera, Tuhin Debnath, Mintu Mondal, Anamitra Mukherjee, Siddhartha Lal, N. Kamaraju
abstract: Fe$_3$GeTe$_2$ is a prototypical metallic van der Waals ferromagnet with itinerant magnetism and a highly tunable Curie temperature, yet how electronic excitations couple to spin and lattice degrees of freedom across its magnetic transition remains largely unexplored. Here, we use two-color pump-probe reflectivity to investigate the coupled electronic, spin, and lattice dynamics. The time-resolved reflectivity exhibits a tri-exponential relaxation, in which the intermediate component shows an anomaly near the Curie temperature due to enhanced interlayer spin-lattice interactions, while the slowest component displays pronounced critical slowing down with an exponent of ~ 0.3, revealing non-universal relaxation dynamics associated with intralayer spin correlations. Furthermore, we observe an emergent nonequilibrium A1g phonon Fano asymmetry that is suppressed in the ferromagnetic phase but anomalously enhanced in the paramagnetic regime, driven by thermally activated anharmonic decay pathways that bridge the kinematic gap to a hot electronic continuum. The pronounced enhancement of the acoustic strain pulse amplitude near T$_c$ further evidences robust magnetoelastic coupling. Overall, our results reveal how magnetic order governs the interplay among critical spin dynamics, electronic continuum excitations, and lattice response in metallic van der Waals ferromagnets
summary: This paper investigates the ultrafast dynamics of the van der Waals ferromagnet Fe3GeTe2 across its magnetic transition. It identifies anomalous, non-universal critical slowing down of spin correlations and demonstrates how thermal acoustic phonons drive an emergent Fano interference between optical phonons and the electronic continuum. These findings highlight the complex interplay between spin, lattice, and electronic degrees of freedom in 2D magnetic materials.
main_problem: Investigating the coupling between electronic, spin, and lattice degrees of freedom across the magnetic phase transition in the van der Waals ferromagnet Fe3GeTe2.
main_result: The study reveals non-universal critical slowing down of intralayer spin dynamics (exponent ~0.3) and the emergence of nonequilibrium Fano interference in the A1g phonon mode in the paramagnetic phase.
method: Two-color ultrafast pump-probe reflectivity spectroscopy combined with a microscopic electron-phonon model and Ginzburg-Landau formalism.
model_or_system: The physical system is Fe3GeTe2 (FGT), a metallic van der Waals ferromagnet. The theoretical framework includes a Hamiltonian describing itinerant electrons, optical phonons, and acoustic phonons, alongside a two-temperature model for electronic and lattice heating.
key_observables: Transient differential reflectivity (delta R/R), relaxation timescales (tau1, tau2, tau3), Fano asymmetry parameter (1/|q|), phonon frequency/linewidth, and acoustic strain pulse amplitude.
important_parameters: Curie temperature (Tc approx 228-230 K), dynamical critical exponent (m approx 0.3), and critical amplitude ratio (R_tau approx 0.6).
main_assumptions: Neglected vertex corrections based on Migdal's theorem; assumed a single-band parabolic electronic dispersion; used a simplified Klemens model for anharmonic decay.
figures_summary: Figures show temperature-dependent magnetization, Raman spectra, tri-exponential relaxation traces, the enhancement of acoustic strain near Tc, and calculated phonon softening and Fano asymmetry as functions of phonon temperature.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the material and problem, describes the pump-probe experimental setup, presents the hierarchy of relaxation timescales and critical slowing down, analyzes the emergent Fano interference and its mechanism, and concludes with the implications for magnetoelastic coupling.
why_interesting: The study explores non-equilibrium phase transitions, non-universal critical dynamics, and the emergence of quantum interference (Fano effect) driven by thermal pathways, which are relevant to many-body dynamics and driven-dissipative systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
